title: Lesson 13
paragraph: Before the school newspaper is printed, students address each reader politely and phone two teachers for comments. They place photos beside the story, post the article online, and wonder how many people will read it. If they regret a word choice, they can roll the page back to an older version. The editor checks that every title sounds clear and that the first story will land on the front page.

words.meaning: to arrive on the ground or in a particular place
words.example: The plane will land at six o'clock.
I will phone my grandmother after dinner.
Please place your bag under the desk.

words.meaning: to feel sorry about something you did or did not do
words.example: I regret sending the message too quickly.

words.meaning: to speak or write to someone in a particular way
words.example: The principal will address the students after lunch.

words.meaning: to produce words or pictures on paper
words.example: Can you print the schedule for me?

words.meaning: to put information online or send something by mail
words.example: The club will post the photos on its website.

words.meaning: to move by turning over and over
words.example: The ball will roll down the hill.

words.meaning: to seem a certain way when heard or described
words.example: Your idea sounds interesting to me.

words.meaning: to think about something because you want to know more
words.japaneseMeaning: 不思議に思う、知りたいと思う
words.example: I wonder why the bus is late today.
